adds.rationale: Fills the geopolitical-energy gap the profile names explicitly (Russia-Ukraine driving oil/gas/LNG); Saudi+Russia extending voluntary production cuts pushed WTI to ~$90 and XLE to a Sept-2023 peak of ~$92.95, a multi-quarter structural setup the current watchlist has zero exposure to.

news_evidence.summary: Saudi Arabia extends voluntary 1 mb/d oil production cut through September, with analysts expecting extension into October; oil prices up ~15% on the month to ~$86/bbl.
Energy ETFs in focus as Saudi cuts likely prolonged; XLE peaked at $92.95 in September, slightly above the $91.25 resistance from November 2022.

removes.rationale: Electron suffered an in-flight anomaly on Sept 19 2023, losing a Capella radar-imaging satellite and grounding the only revenue-generating launch vehicle pending an FAA mishap investigation; the return-to-flight timeline is uncertain and this is a clean fundamental thesis breach pre-as-of, so step out and revisit after the cause is identified and Electron returns to cadence.

news_evidence.summary: Rocket Lab's Electron suffered its first failure in over two years, losing a Capella radar-imaging satellite ~2.5 minutes into flight; FAA mishap investigation required before return-to-flight.
Radar-imaging satellite lost as Rocket Lab Electron rocket suffers launch failure; Rocket Lab's first launch failure since May 2021 ends a 20-consecutive-success streak.

rationale_overall: One-for-one swap that fills the profile's named geopolitical-energy gap (XLE captures the Saudi-cut/Russia-Ukraine oil-and-gas tailwind the current watchlist has zero exposure to) while stepping back from RKLB after its Sept 19 Electron failure grounded its only revenue-generating launch vehicle. Net effect: watchlist stays at cap (8), rockets/spacecraft exposure pauses pending RKLB's return-to-flight, geopolitical-commodities wave gets its first seat, and the AI core (MSFT/GOOGL/NVDA) plus nuclear (URA), quantum (IONQ), demographics (LLY), and robotics-via-healthcare (ISRG) all stay intact.
